description: Testing every pair of entities for collision each frame, so cost grows quadratically and frame time collapses at scale.
body: Failure mode: collision runs a nested loop comparing all entity pairs, so doubling entities quadruples the work and frame time degrades sharply as the world fills. Detection signals: frame rate fine with few objects but collapsing as entity count rises; profiler shows the collision system dominating; cost tracks n^2. Do not brute-force all pairs. Safer fallback: add a broad phase (uniform grid, quadtree/octree, or sweep-and-prune) to cull to nearby candidate pairs, then run narrow-phase tests only on those, and confirm collision cost scales well below n^2.
